summary:
The provided evidence does not contain any information about the genetic inheritance of achondroplasia or the likelihood of unaffected children. Despite widespread medical knowledge indicating a 75% chance of an unaffected baby when both parents have achondroplasia (due to autosomal dominant inheritance with each parent being a carrier), this detail is missing entirely from the source materials.  

Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{(Not determinable from evidence)}}$ 

Note: This summary focuses only on clinically relevant points derived directly from available content—excluding external assumptions beyond stated facts.
